findings.problem: The chapter's biggest reveal becomes explanatory before it becomes singular. Once Maren reaches the fissure, the prose quickly names the other side as wild, pre-classified reality and translates the System into dam and maintenance language, but it never gives one irreducibly specific glimpse of what that reality actually is. The beat is legible, but it reads like cosmology summary instead of a once-in-the-book encounter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the beyond-the-wall reveal centered on snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:125 so Maren encounters one or two concrete, impossible particulars before he abstracts the System into dam and wall language. Keep the containment logic, but let the explanation around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:127 arrive as inference after sensory evidence rather than as the first rendering of the thing itself.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised reveal anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:121 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123, a cold reader can point to at least one concrete phenomenon behind the wall that is not just a label like wild or unclassified, and one bodily effect it has on Maren before the passage explains the containment theory. If the reader's only takeaway is the abstract concept, the revision fails.

findings.problem: The required state shift is Maren choosing maintenance over destruction, but the text does not stage destruction as a live option while he is in contact with the crack. He seals the Woundmouth first, then Aldric tells him afterward that he could have broken it. That makes the defining choice retrospective certification rather than dramatized action.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add the fork inside the consumption sequence around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:129, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:133. While Maren is at the fissure, make the outward push versus inward sealing legible as two different actions or impulses, with an immediate cost or temptation attached to each. Then trim or reframe the aftermath exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:179 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:187 so Aldric is confirming the consequence of a choice already seen, not introducing the existence of the choice.
findings.acceptance_test: In revised text anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:129, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:137, the reader can identify the destructive option, the sealing option, and the moment Maren actively commits to one before he collapses. If Aldric's question at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:187 still reveals the choice for the first time, the revision fails.

findings.problem: By the time the chapter reaches Maren being both crack and sealant, the wall-thesis has already landed. The aftermath then re-proves the same point in cleaner abstract language through the filing-system explanation, the maintenance-worker framing, and the meta comparison to how stories usually stage heroic choices. The scene keeps translating its meaning instead of turning again.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or retool the aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:203, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:213. Keep the must-land beats that need to remain on page: Maren survives, he has sealed part of the wound, he commits to the work, and Aldric identifies other cracks. Any retained material in this span should introduce a new cost, limit, or operational consequence rather than restating the wall thesis in cleaner terms.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised aftermath anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:191, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:223, once the text has established that Maren sealed part of the fracture and will continue, the remaining scene adds at least one new operational fact or concrete cost before the mission handoff. If the same wall, dam, maintenance, or filing-system idea is paraphrased across multiple later beats without new consequence, the revision fails.

findings.problem: The chapter's central exchange makes Maren sound too composed for both the moment and his style-bible voice profile. "Because someone has to do the maintenance" lands as an aphoristic mic-drop, the follow-up becomes a mini-speech diagnosing the world, and his self-definition arrives in finished thesis sentences. That is composed dialogue: thesis-speech, quotable line, mini-speech diagnosis, and tidy self-explanation in a moment where Maren should be shorter, rougher, and more self-interrupting under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite Maren's side of the conversation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:199, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:203, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:211 so the same decision comes through pressure-true speech: immediate bodily detail from the consumption, at least one self-correction or abandoned clause, and more push-pull with Aldric instead of a single uninterrupted explanation. Preserve Aldric as an interlocutor rather than an audience for Maren's thesis.
findings.acceptance_test: In revised dialogue across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:199, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:211, Maren does not get a standalone quotable thesis line, and he visibly revises or breaks off at least once while talking about his own role. At least half of his lines in this exchange should be grounded in immediate physical or practical detail rather than abstract cosmology. If the passage still reads like a post-battle speech, the revision fails.

findings.problem: One marked sentence scaffold recurs often enough to feel automatic: emphatic negation followed by correction or restatement. The chapter reaches for the same Not X. Not Y. rhythm in the reveal, the dying beat, the recovery, and the closing exchange, so different moments start landing with the same manufactured emphasis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:101, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:221. Keep emphasis where needed, but replace several current negation pivots with direct image, cause-and-effect syntax, or noun-first observation so each beat earns its own cadence.
findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised passages anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:101, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:221, no more than two emphatic Not-based correction beats remain, and the others are replaced by distinct sentence shapes that still make the local contrast legible. If adjacent revelation beats still lean on the same binary-negation rhythm, the revision fails.

summary: Continuity, tense, and ritual legibility hold, and the Woundmouth sequence renders bodily consequence with enough on-page specificity to avoid sanitization. The chapter still fails pass level in all four lenses because its central cosmological reveal stays too diagrammatic, its decisive maintenance-versus-destruction choice is introduced retrospectively instead of dramatized in the act, the aftermath re-explains what the revelation already established, Maren's key exchange turns into composed thesis-speech, and the prose leans too often on the same negation-and-correction scaffold.
